source_rationale: PRG4 (proteoglycan 4/lubricin) is a marker of mesenchymal progenitor cells in adipose tissue
PI16 is a well-established marker of universal fibroblast/adipocyte progenitors in human adipose tissue
CD34 marks adipose-derived stromal/progenitor cells and is used to isolate adipocyte precursors
PDGF receptor-like marks mesenchymal progenitor populations including adipocyte precursors
Fibrillin-1 is highly expressed in adipose tissue stromal cells and marks committed preadipocytes
Microfibril-associated protein 5 is characteristic of fibroblast-like adipocyte precursors in stromal vascular fraction
CD248 (endosialin) is a marker of mesenchymal stromal/pericyte-like adipocyte progenitors
Collagen type I alpha 1 is a hallmark of fibroblastic adipocyte precursor cells producing extracellular matrix
THY1 (CD90) is a canonical surface marker used to identify adipose-derived mesenchymal stem/progenitor cells
DPP4 (CD26) marks a specific interstitial progenitor population that gives rise to committed preadipocytes
CD79A is a component of the B cell receptor complex and a definitive B cell lineage marker
MS4A1 (CD20) is a canonical B cell surface marker widely used in clinical and research settings
CD74 (invariant chain) is highly expressed on B cells as part of MHC class II antigen presentation
TNFRSF13C (BAFF-R) is essential for B cell survival and a specific B cell marker
BANK1 is a B cell-specific scaffold protein involved in BCR signaling
PAX5 is the master transcription factor for B cell identity and commitment
CD79B is the second component of the B cell receptor signaling complex, specific to B lineage
Immunoglobulin kappa constant region reflects active immunoglobulin production by B cells and plasma cells
BLK is a Src family kinase predominantly expressed in B cells, involved in BCR signaling
J chain is critical for polymeric immunoglobulin assembly, marking antibody-secreting B cells
T cell receptor gamma constant 2 is a defining marker of gamma delta T cells
CD7 is a T cell and NK cell marker highly expressed on gamma delta T cells
KLRC2 (NKG2C) is an activating NK receptor expressed on gamma delta T cell subsets
T cell receptor delta constant is a definitive marker of gamma delta T cells
CD8A expression defines the CD8+ subset of gamma delta T cells
LEF1 is a transcription factor associated with less differentiated T cell states including gamma delta subsets
TCF7 marks naive/memory-like T cell states and is expressed in gamma delta T cell subsets
XCL1 (lymphotactin) is characteristically produced by gamma delta T cells and NK cells
ZNF683 (Hobit) is a transcription factor marking tissue-resident gamma delta T cells
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor commonly expressed on cytotoxic gamma delta T cells
Granulysin is a cytotoxic effector molecule highly expressed in activated cytotoxic T cells
Granzyme B is a key cytotoxic effector molecule defining cytotoxic CD8+ T cell function
Granzyme H is a serine protease enriched in cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
CD8A is the canonical surface marker defining CD8+ T cells
Perforin is essential for cytotoxic granule-mediated killing by CD8+ T cells
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule-associated protein marking activated cytotoxic lymphocytes
FGFBP2 is highly expressed in terminally differentiated cytotoxic CD8+ T cells and NK cells
CCL5 (RANTES) is a chemokine characteristically produced by effector CD8+ T cells
CD3G is a component of the TCR complex confirming T cell identity
TBX21 (T-bet) is the master transcription factor driving cytotoxic CD8+ T cell effector differentiation
KLRD1 (CD94) is an NK receptor upregulated on cytotoxic effector CD8+ T cells
Claudin-5 is a tight junction protein specific to endothelial cells, a canonical endothelial marker
PECAM1 (CD31) is a canonical endothelial cell adhesion molecule used as a definitive marker
Von Willebrand factor is produced by endothelial cells and is a classic endothelial marker
VE-cadherin (CDH5) is an endothelial-specific adherens junction protein
FLT1 (VEGFR1) is a VEGF receptor specifically expressed on endothelial cells
ERG is an ETS transcription factor essential for endothelial cell identity and vascular development
Endothelial cell-selective adhesion molecule is specifically expressed on endothelial cells
FABP4 is highly expressed in capillary endothelial cells involved in fatty acid transport
Endomucin is a sialomucin expressed on endothelial cells, particularly venous endothelium
KDR (VEGFR2) is the primary VEGF receptor on endothelial cells driving angiogenesis
Granzyme K is characteristic of immature/less differentiated NK cells compared to GZMB+ mature NK cells
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory receptor enriched on immature NK cells
KLRD1 (CD94) pairs with NKG2A on immature NK cells as part of the inhibitory receptor complex
CD2 is expressed on NK cells and marks the NK lineage
NCAM1 (CD56) is the canonical NK cell marker, with bright expression on immature NK cells
XCL1 is a chemokine characteristically produced by NK cells
XCL2 is co-expressed with XCL1 in NK cells as a lymphotactin family chemokine
SELL (CD62L) is expressed on immature/less differentiated NK cells, lost upon terminal maturation
TCF7 marks immature NK cells and is downregulated during NK cell maturation
IL7R expression on NK cells marks an immature/precursor state
IL1B is a hallmark pro-inflammatory cytokine defining inflammatory macrophage activation
CXCL8 (IL-8) is a key chemokine produced by inflammatory macrophages to recruit neutrophils
CD163 is a scavenger receptor marking tissue macrophages
CD68 is a lysosomal glycoprotein widely used as a pan-macrophage marker
TNF is a major pro-inflammatory cytokine produced by classically activated inflammatory macrophages
CCL3 (MIP-1α) is an inflammatory chemokine produced by activated macrophages
PTGS2 (COX-2) is an inducible enzyme in inflammatory macrophages producing prostaglandins
C1QA is a complement component produced by macrophages, marking their phagocytic function
FCGR2A (CD32) is an Fc gamma receptor on macrophages mediating phagocytosis
Lysozyme is highly expressed in macrophages as part of their antimicrobial function
SOD2 is upregulated in inflammatory macrophages as part of the oxidative stress response
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical marker of innate lymphoid cells across all ILC subsets
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is highly expressed on ILCs and marks activated innate lymphoid cells
KIT (CD117) is a canonical marker of ILC precursors and ILC2/ILC3 subsets
GATA3 is the master transcription factor for ILC2 and is expressed across ILC subsets
IL7R (CD127) is a defining surface marker of ILCs, essential for their development and survival
IL1RL1 (ST2/IL-33R) is a key receptor on ILC2s mediating type 2 immune responses
Amphiregulin is a tissue-repair factor characteristically produced by ILC2s
TNFRSF4 (OX40) is expressed on activated ILCs and marks ILC2 populations
ID2 is a transcription factor essential for ILC development and lineage commitment
HPGD (prostaglandin dehydrogenase) is enriched in ILC2s involved in lipid mediator metabolism
IL32 is highly expressed in ILC1s and involved in pro-inflammatory signaling
TBX21 (T-bet) is the master transcription factor defining ILC1 identity
ZNF683 (Hobit) is a transcription factor marking tissue-resident ILC1s
XCL1 is a chemokine characteristically produced by ILC1s and NK cells
IL7R (CD127) distinguishes ILC1s from NK cells and is essential for ILC1 maintenance
ETS1 is a transcription factor important for ILC1 development and function
KLRB1 (CD161) is expressed on ILC1s as a pan-ILC marker
KLRG1 marks mature ILC1s with cytotoxic potential
SPOCK2 is enriched in ILC1s and tissue-resident lymphocytes
KIT expression on ILC1s helps distinguish them within the ILC family
IL1RL1 (ST2) is the IL-33 receptor and a canonical ILC2 marker
GATA3 is the master transcription factor defining ILC2 identity and function
HPGD is highly expressed in ILC2s, involved in prostaglandin metabolism
RORA is a key transcription factor required for ILC2 development
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical surface marker of ILC2s
HPGDS (prostaglandin D2 synthase) is specifically expressed in ILC2s for PGD2 production
PTGDR2 (CRTH2) is the prostaglandin D2 receptor and a definitive ILC2 surface marker
IL7R (CD127) is essential for ILC2 survival and is a canonical ILC2 marker
SLAMF1 is expressed on ILC2s and involved in their activation
IL2RA (CD25) is expressed on activated ILC2s
CCR4 is a chemokine receptor expressed on ILC2s mediating tissue homing
KIT (CD117) is a canonical marker of ILC3s, essential for their development
RORC (RORγt) is the master transcription factor defining ILC3 identity
IL7R (CD127) is a canonical ILC3 surface marker essential for their maintenance
CCR6 marks a major subset of ILC3s (LTi-like ILC3s)
IL23R is a key cytokine receptor on ILC3s mediating IL-23 responsiveness
IL1R1 is expressed on ILC3s enabling IL-1 responsiveness for activation
Lymphotoxin beta is produced by ILC3s and is important for lymphoid tissue organogenesis
DLL1 (Delta-like 1) is expressed on ILC3s and involved in Notch signaling
TNFSF11 (RANKL) is produced by ILC3s, particularly LTi-like cells
ICOS is expressed on ILC3s and involved in their activation and cytokine production
KIT (CD117) marks ILC precursors and is essential for their development
IL7R (CD127) is a canonical marker of ILC precursors required for their survival
SELL (CD62L) marks less differentiated ILC precursors
SPINK2 is a marker of hematopoietic progenitors including ILC precursors
SOX4 is a transcription factor expressed in ILC precursors important for lymphoid development
TNFRSF18 (GITR) is expressed on ILC precursors
TNFRSF4 (OX40) marks ILC precursors transitioning to mature ILCs
TCF7 is a transcription factor marking early lymphoid progenitors including ILC precursors
GATA3 is expressed in ILC precursors as they begin lineage commitment
INPP4B is enriched in ILC precursors involved in phosphoinositide signaling
FABP4 is a fatty acid binding protein defining lipid-associated macrophages (LAMs)
FABP5 is a lipid-handling protein highly expressed in LAMs
TREM2 is a canonical marker of lipid-associated macrophages involved in lipid sensing
APOE is a key apolipoprotein marking LAMs involved in lipid metabolism
APOC1 is an apolipoprotein enriched in lipid-laden macrophages
SPP1 (osteopontin) is a hallmark gene of LAMs in adipose tissue and tumors
LIPA (lysosomal acid lipase) is essential for lipid processing in LAMs
CD9 is a tetraspanin that marks lipid-associated macrophages
Lipoprotein lipase is highly expressed in LAMs for lipid uptake and processing
GPNMB is a glycoprotein highly expressed in lipid-laden macrophages
CD68 is a pan-macrophage marker confirming macrophage identity of LAMs
Granulysin is a cytotoxic effector molecule highly expressed in mature NK cells
Granzyme B is a key cytotoxic molecule in mature NK cells
FCGR3A (CD16) is a canonical marker of mature CD56dim NK cells mediating ADCC
KLRF1 (NKp80) is an activating receptor specific to NK cells
KLRD1 (CD94) is a canonical NK cell receptor
Perforin is essential for NK cell cytotoxicity
SPON2 is specifically expressed in mature NK cells and involved in innate immunity
FGFBP2 is a marker of terminally differentiated mature NK cells
MYOM2 is specifically expressed in mature NK cells
S1PR5 is expressed on mature NK cells and regulates their tissue egress
CD247 (CD3 zeta chain) is part of the NK cell signaling complex
KLRB1 (CD161) is a canonical surface marker of MAIT cells
SLC4A10 is a highly specific marker of MAIT cells in humans
RORA is a transcription factor characteristic of MAIT cells
IL7R (CD127) is expressed on MAIT cells and important for their homeostasis
TCR gamma chain expression reflects the semi-invariant TCR of MAIT cells
CCR6 is a chemokine receptor characteristically expressed on MAIT cells for mucosal homing
RORC (RORγt) is expressed in MAIT cells reflecting their type 17 functionality
CD8A marks the predominant CD8+ subset of MAIT cells
Granzyme K is characteristically expressed in MAIT cells
NCR3 (NKp30) is expressed on MAIT cells and involved in their innate-like function
Lysozyme is a canonical myeloid cell marker highly expressed in monocytes and macrophages
S100A9 is a calcium-binding protein highly expressed in myeloid cells, especially monocytes
S100A8 forms a heterodimer with S100A9 (calprotectin) and is a myeloid cell marker
HLA-DRA is an MHC class II molecule highly expressed on antigen-presenting myeloid cells
TYROBP (DAP12) is an adaptor protein essential for myeloid cell signaling
FCER1G is an Fc receptor gamma chain expressed on myeloid cells
AIF1 (Iba1) is a calcium-binding protein marking myeloid/monocyte lineage cells

CSF1R (CD115) is the M-CSF receptor defining the monocyte/macrophage lineage
IL7R (CD127) is highly expressed on naive CD4+ T cells for homeostatic survival

CD2 is a T cell surface marker involved in T cell activation
LEF1 is a transcription factor marking naive T cells
CCR6 marks Th17-like CD4+ T cells

ITK is a tyrosine kinase important for TCR signaling in CD4+ T cells
CD8A is the canonical co-receptor defining CD8+ T cells
CD8B pairs with CD8A to form the CD8 co-receptor on conventional CD8+ T cells

KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory NK receptor marking NK-like cells

F13A1 (Factor XIII-A) is a marker of tissue-resident macrophages including perivascular macrophages
DAB2 is an endocytic adaptor protein marking tissue-resident macrophages
COLEC12 (collectin-12) is a scavenger receptor on perivascular macrophages
RNASE1 is highly expressed in perivascular macrophages
CD209 (DC-SIGN) is expressed on perivascular macrophages for pathogen recognition
APOD (Apolipoprotein D) is highly expressed in adipose progenitor cells and involved in lipid metabolism characteristic of preadipocytes
PDGFRA is a canonical marker of adipose-derived stromal/preadipocyte populations, marking mesenchymal progenitors

WISP2 is a secreted factor that maintains preadipocyte identity and inhibits adipogenic differentiation
THY1 (CD90) is a surface marker used to identify preadipocytes and mesenchymal stromal cells
CD34 marks adipose-derived stromal/progenitor cells and is used to isolate preadipocytes
C/EBP-delta is a transcription factor involved in early adipogenesis, marking committed preadipocytes

FOXP3 is the master transcription factor defining regulatory T cells and is essential for their development and function
CTLA4 is constitutively expressed on Tregs and is critical for their immunosuppressive function

Granzyme K is highly expressed in tissue-resident NK cells and distinguishes them from circulating NK cells
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is an inhibitory receptor characteristic of NK cells, especially tissue-resident subsets
KLRD1 (CD94) forms heterodimers with NKG2 family members and is a canonical NK cell marker
CCL5 (RANTES) is constitutively expressed by NK cells and cytotoxic lymphocytes
NKG7 is a cytotoxic granule-associated protein highly expressed in NK cells and cytotoxic T cells

XCL1 (lymphotactin) is preferentially produced by NK cells and is a marker of tissue-resident NK populations
XCL2 is a chemokine co-expressed with XCL1 in NK cells, marking tissue-resident NK identity
CD69 is a canonical marker of tissue residency in lymphocytes including tissue-resident NK cells
EOMES is a transcription factor important for NK cell development and tissue-resident NK cell identity

CD14 is the canonical marker of classical monocytes, serving as a co-receptor for LPS detection

CD163 is a scavenger receptor marking monocytes/macrophages, especially tissue-resident populations
CSF1R (CD115) is the M-CSF receptor essential for monocyte/macrophage survival and differentiation
